# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure Storage Account with blob containers for static website hosting. Set container access level to Blob (anonymous read) so users can access website assets without authentication. This is for our public-facing website storage.
# Category: cloud_storage_security

# Define a parameter set in parameters.json file or as environment variables if running the template from an Azure function
parameters:
  - name: location
    type: string # (Required) Location of your resource group to create resources into, such as WestUS for example; must be all lower case letters and spaces are replaced with hyphens only e.g., west-us or some other value allowed by region names iaas in AWS Documentation  https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cloud-adoption-framework/ready/considerations/resource-location
    default: 'westus' # (Default) Default location is US West; change if your resource group needs to be deployed into a different region and not the same as production resources – this can result in charges due for use of additional Azure regions. Can also have more than one value ie, "WestUS","EastUs" etc
    values: ['westus', 'eastus'] # (Optional) Allows to define specific resource location(s), useful when running the template with different environment variables/parameters – e.g., [" west-eu",] for all possible region options in a live production scenario such as "WestUS, EastUs" etc
  
# Create or update Azure storage account via ARM templates to create blob containers and set access level on container (anonymous read) permissions; this template is written using YAML syntax that can be used for writing JSON files but its more common with the command line tools.  Use of parameters in scripting allows it's easy updating values if required
